title: Question 4.2 (Barba, Pilz, and Schnider)
statement_text: What is the average complexity of the $k$-level in an arrangement of great-circles on the sphere when the south pole is chosen uniformly at random among all cells?
context_text: The paper states: 'This question was raised by Barba, Pilz, and Schnider while sharing a pizza [BPS19, Question 4.2].' The paper investigates the expected size of the $k$-level in a simple arrangement of $n$ great-circles under this random model. The question is motivated by the well-studied worst-case complexity of the $k$-level and the correspondence between great-circle arrangements on the sphere and line arrangements in the plane.
paper_contribution: Proves an upper bound of $O((k+1)^2)$ on the expected complexity of the $k$-level (specifically, at most $4e\cdot(k+2)^2$) when the south pole is chosen uniformly at random among all cells, and this bound is independent of the number $n$ of great-circles.
notes: PDF source. The exact verbatim statement of Question 4.2 from [BPS19] is not reproduced in this paper; the statement above is reconstructed from context. Math notation may be garbled.
paper_title: On the Average Complexity of the $k$-Level
